— · — · Singapore

Oasia Hotel Downtown, Singapore. Photo: Far East Hospitality Trust

Latest Hospitality Earnings · REIT Update · Quarterly · Hospitality · Multi-Geography · Capital Management

Far East H-Trust 1Q 2026: 84.2% Occupancy, but ADR -3.9% Underneath — Japan FPN +26.6% GOP, WACD Drops to 2.3%

Far East Hospitality Trust 1Q 2026 (quarter ended 31 March 2026, reported 30 April 2026) printed gross revenue +11.3% to S$28.1M and income available for distribution +12.2% to S$17.9M — but the operational picture is more nuanced than the headlines suggest. Singapore hotel occupancy jumped 5.2 percentage points to 84.2% on the back of the biennial Singapore Airshow and an earlier Hari Raya, yet ADR fell 3.9% to S$165 — RevPAR grew just 2.5%, with the trade-off between occupancy and rate the cleanest read in the quarter. The Japan FPN acquisition delivered its first full comparable quarter with RevPAR +10.8% and GOP +26.6%, validating the diversification thesis flagged in the FY2025 update. Average cost of debt compressed a further 80bps from year-end to 2.3% — doing most of the heavy lifting on the +12.2% distribution growth.

4 May 2026 · 1,888 words · 9 min read
Read the full entry →

More from this week

Updated 4 May 2026

W Singapore – Sentosa Cove. Photo: CDL Hospitality Trusts

Hospitality

CDLHT 1Q 2026: Singapore Strength Funds The Geographic Drag, Capital Stack Re-Rated To 2.8% / 35.3% Gearing — But Maldives And Japan Mark The Real Geopolitical Exposure

Singapore Hotels delivered NPI +5.9% on Airshow-driven RevPAR S$184 (+6.6%) — the optical headline. The structural read is two independent geopolitical drags: Maldives NPI −26.3%, Japan NPI −10.3%. UK Living Assets NPI +68.8% doing stability work; S$250M perpetual issuance dropped WACD to 2.8% / gearing to 35.3%.

4 May 2026 · 11 min read

Ngee Ann City, Singapore. Photo: Starhill Global REIT

Commercial

Starhill Global 3Q FY25/26: Master Lease Moat Now 54.3% Of GRI, Toshin Renewed To 2043 With June 2028 Rent Review — Reported NPI Flat But +1.2% Ex-Divestment, Three Step-Up Catalysts Through Mid-2028

The 0.0% NPI print is the master-lease structure working as designed — 54.3% of GRI on contracted step-ups means the trough between reviews looks flat, while three forward catalysts (David Jones Perth Aug 2026, Wisma Atria façade mid-2026, Toshin June 2028) anchor the FY26/27-FY28/29 income line.

3 May 2026 · 11 min read

Sun Plaza, Medan, North Sumatera. Photo: Landmark REIT (formerly Lippo Malls Indonesia Retail Trust)

Commercial

Landmark REIT 1Q 2026: Rebrand From LMIRT Lands With +13.6% IDR Rental Growth, 87.5% Occupancy, Gearing Down To 40.22% — But Distribution Suspension Continues For 7th Consecutive Cessation On 2016 Perpetuals

Operating recovery is real (NPI +15.6% IDR, occupancy 9pp above industry, 70% of malls above 85%), the rights issue cleaned the balance sheet (gearing −332bps to 40.22%, US$22.6m 2026 Notes redeemed) — but distributions stay suspended for Unitholders and both perpetual tranches pending further cashflow improvement.

2 May 2026 · 11 min read

Recent coverage

May 2026

Kingsland 21, Spennymoor, UK. Photo: Stoneweg Europe Stapled Trust

Industrial

SERT 1Q 2026: +1.5% DPS Growth, +41% AiOnX Revaluation, €85M Redeployed In One Quarter, 15%-25% Data Centre Allocation Target By FY2028 — At 0.78x P/NAV And 8.6% Yield

Reported NPI down 1.3% on completed 2025 divestments — but like-for-like NPI is up 2.3%, the original €50m AiOnX equity stake has been written up 41% inside twelve months, €85m was redeployed in 1Q at a 6.7% blended yield, and the data centre allocation target has been hardened to 15%–25% by FY2028.

2 May 2026 · 11 min read

Berlin Campus, Germany. Photo: IREIT Global

Commercial

IREIT 1Q2026: Darmstadt Letting Traction Lands, German Refi Cost Hits 4.1%, Berlin Phase 2 Anchor Decision By 3Q2026

Portfolio committed occupancy lifts to 92.2% ex-Berlin on three new Darmstadt leases (c.9,070 sqm, 8.3-year WAULT) — operationally the cleanest quarter the German book has had in years. Weighted average interest rate stepped from 2.8% to 4.1% on the Oct-2025 German refi swaps, ICR dropped to 2.3x, Berlin Phase 2 anchor decision targeted by 3Q2026.

1 May 2026 · 9 min read

April 2026

Toa Payoh North 1, Singapore. Photo: Mapletree Industrial Trust

Industrial

MIT 4Q & FY25/26: Singapore Compounding Quietly At +6.2% Reversions, S$535.3M Divestment Lands +22.1% Above Cost — But North American Data Centre Cracks Are Widening And Two May 2026 Non-Renewals Are On The Cards

Three SG flatted-factory clusters divested 15 Aug 2025 at +22.1% above original cost. Singapore retained book compounding cleanly: 93.4% occupancy, +6.2% rental reversion, 84.7% tenant retention. North America DC occupancy 86.1% (down from 87.5%), AT&T San Diego and Global Colocation Hawthorne flagged for May 2026 non-renewal.

28 April 2026 · 11 min read

VivoCity, Singapore. Photo: Mapletree Pan Asia Commercial Trust

Commercial

MPACT FY25/26: VivoCity Compounding +5.0% Reversions, S$1.05B Office Disposals, Hong Kong Reshaping Quietly Into Singapore Anchor — But North America DC Cracks Are The Open Risk

VivoCity rental reversion +5.0%, FY25/26 occupancy 99.5%; Hong Kong fell from 41% of revenue to 27% post-disposal; pro-forma gearing 36.4% versus 39.6% reported. The portfolio is reshaping cleanly toward Singapore — and the question becomes whether that pivot can absorb FY27 retail supply.

28 April 2026 · 10 min read

Changi Logistics Centre, Singapore. Photo: CapitaLand Ascendas REIT

Industrial

CLAR 1Q 2026: Reversions +14.6% Headline Mask The Singapore +12.3% / US -3.5% Geographic Bifurcation; Welwyn Garden City Acquisition + S$220M Aperia Redevelopment Lock In Forward Pipeline

Headline rental reversion +14.6% portfolio-wide masks a sharper geographic split: Singapore +12.3%, Australia +21.0%, UK/Europe +8.6%, US -3.5%. Aperia Singapore S$220M redevelopment + Welwyn Garden City UK acquisition lock the forward pipeline; +14.6% reversion + 92.6% occupancy + 38.0% gearing.

27 April 2026 · 10 min read

Company Unit Industry (SSIC) Type Status UEN Reg. Date
💡 Prospecting Intelligence
Manual Address Lookup
-- 1. Create table in Supabase SQL editor
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_registry (
  uen TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  entity_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  street_name TEXT,
  blk_no TEXT,
  unit_no TEXT,
  postal_code TEXT,
  entity_type TEXT,
  entity_status TEXT,
  primary_ssic_code TEXT,
  primary_ssic_desc TEXT,
  reg_date DATE,
  last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_tenant_street ON tenant_registry(street_name, blk_no);
CREATE INDEX idx_tenant_postal ON tenant_registry(postal_code);
-- 2. Download ACRA CSV from data.gov.sg
-- https://data.gov.sg/datasets/d_3f960c10fed6145404ca7b821f263b87/view
-- Filter: street_name IS NOT NULL (industrial addresses)
-- 3. Enable Row Level Security + public read policy
ALTER TABLE tenant_registry ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY "public_read" ON tenant_registry FOR SELECT USING (true);
-- 4. Monthly refresh (run via cron or Supabase scheduled function)
-- Truncate + re-import from latest ACRA CSV download

Live C&I Market Data

Before you continue —
ATLAS AI answers questions using Singapore commercial & industrial transaction data from URA REALIS (1995–2026). It queries live data and uses AI to interpret results.

This is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial, investment, or legal advice. Data reflects URA caveated transactions and may not capture all market activity.
